name: Green Chili, Doc Martin's
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Poblano peppers, roasted and cored
2 small Tomatoes, peeled, seeded, and chopped
recipeInstructions:
In a skillet, brown the ground chuck over moderately high heat. Transfer it to a large pot using a slotted spoon. In the skillet, brown the pork and sirloin separately, then transfer them to the pot, discarding any fat from the skillet.
Add 3/4 cup butter to the skillet and cook onion, garlic, and coriander over moderately low heat until the onion is softened. Add Tabasco, oregano, cumin, parsley, black pepper, and 1 tablespoon flour, stirring constantly for a few minutes.
Combine this mixture with the pot containing broth, beer, roasted poblanos, and tomatoes. Bring to a boil, then simmer occasionally for 2 hours. Knead together the remaining 3 tablespoons butter and flour to make beurre manie. Add it gradually to the pot while stirring, then simmer the chili for an additional 5 minutes.
Serves about 10-12.
